metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Instrumental Music 12 is the capstone senior instrumental music course, developing students' highest level of musical artistry, technical proficiency, and musical leadership. Students engage in independent musical decision-making, perform demanding repertoire from diverse traditions, and contribute meaningfully to ensemble culture. First Peoples musical traditions and contemporary Indigenous artists are studied with depth and respect. This course prepares students for post-secondary music programs, conservatory study, and lifelong musical engagement.
metadata.general_description: The capstone senior instrumental music course developing the highest level of musical artistry, technical proficiency, independent musicianship, and ensemble leadership — preparing students for post-secondary music programs and lifelong musical engagement.

big_ideas.statement: Musical artistry integrates technical mastery, expressive depth, and cultural understanding.
big_ideas.elaboration: At the highest level, musicianship is not just technical — it requires emotional intelligence, stylistic sensitivity, and an understanding of the cultural contexts that give music its deepest meaning.

big_ideas.statement: Musicians are cultural stewards who carry and transmit musical traditions.
big_ideas.elaboration: Learning and performing music from diverse traditions carries a responsibility — to understand where the music comes from, who created it, and what it means to the communities for whom it is most deeply significant.

big_ideas.statement: Sustained musical development requires commitment, critical self-reflection, and artistic courage.
big_ideas.elaboration: Reaching the highest levels of musicianship demands years of consistent, reflective practice — combined with the ongoing willingness to challenge oneself, seek feedback, and grow beyond current technical and expressive limits.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate the highest level of technical proficiency and expressive musicianship; perform demanding and diverse repertoire with artistic independence; create through improvisation and composition.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate the highest level of technical proficiency and expressive musicianship on my instrument.
I can perform demanding and diverse repertoire with artistic independence and stylistic authenticity.
I can create original music through improvisation, arrangement, and composition with musical sophistication.

competencies.raw: Critically analyze musical works and own performances with sophistication; lead musical reflection and feedback; examine music in deep cultural, historical, and social context.
i_can_statements: I can critically analyze musical works and my own performances with sophisticated musical vocabulary and cultural awareness.
I can lead musical reflection and provide specific, constructive feedback that strengthens ensemble work.
I can examine music in its deepest cultural, historical, and social contexts — understanding what music does in the world.

competencies.raw: Perform at the highest artistic level in formal concert contexts; develop a professional-quality musical portfolio; communicate musical understanding and artistic decisions.
i_can_statements: I can perform at the highest artistic level in formal concert, solo, and chamber contexts.
I can develop a professional-quality musical portfolio documenting technical and artistic development.
I can clearly communicate my musical understanding, interpretive decisions, and performance insights.

competencies.raw: Lead by example in ensemble culture and musical community; engage with First Peoples musical traditions with depth and respect; prepare for post-secondary music pathways.
i_can_statements: I can lead by example in ensemble culture — demonstrating professionalism, generosity, and musical leadership.
I can engage with First Peoples musical traditions with depth and cultural respect.
I can prepare thoughtfully for post-secondary music auditions, programs, and career pathways.

areas.name: Capstone Technical Development
topics: Advanced repertoire study: concertos, sonatas, or major orchestral/band literature
Extended technique and specialized techniques appropriate to instrument
Comprehensive scale and technical study at advanced level
Sight-reading at Grade 6-8 RCM/ABRSM or equivalent
Recording and self-analysis: audio and video review for targeted improvement

areas.name: Solo and Chamber Performance
areas.raw: Solo repertoire, chamber music, and recital preparation.
topics: Preparing and performing a substantial solo program
Chamber music: rehearsing and performing in small ensembles
Recital preparation: programming, practice strategy, and performance preparation
Performance anxiety management: mental preparation and performance routines
Post-recital reflection and documentation

areas.name: Ensemble Leadership
areas.raw: Advanced ensemble performance and musical leadership.
topics: Section leadership: tuning, stylistic guidance, and ensemble coordination
Contributing to rehearsal efficiency: preparation, initiative, and musical problem-solving
Conducting and score reading: basic conducting techniques and full score literacy
Community performance: planning and leading concerts for community audiences
Mentoring younger musicians

areas.name: Music Studies and Post-Secondary Preparation
areas.raw: Music history, theory, and preparation for post-secondary music pathways.
topics: Advanced music theory: post-tonal harmony, jazz harmony, and contemporary notation
First Peoples musical traditions and contemporary Indigenous composers
Music research: writing a substantial analytical paper on a musical topic
Post-secondary audition preparation: repertoire selection, live and recorded auditions
Career pathways: performance, education, therapy, production, and arts administration
